{"path":"<FILE_PATH>","content":"# Vertical Search AI Solutions for the Fashion Industry

## Comprehensive Market Report

---

## Executive Summary

The fashion industry has become a primary beneficiary of vertical AI solutions, with specialized platforms addressing the entire value chain from trend forecasting through post-purchase. This report identifies key players, documents deployment case studies, analyzes pricing models, and maps solutions to fashion value chain stages.

---

## 1. Solution Profiles

### 1.1 ViSenze

**Overview:** ViSenze provides an AI-powered product discovery platform encompassing visual search, recommendations, and automated tagging. The company was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in Singapore with operations globally.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

- **Myntra** (India): Leverages ViSenze's "Similar" and "Look" recommendation engines to suggest visually similar items and recommend shoppable products from model images, resulting in increased average order value and revenue. The visual image search adoption increased 35% year-over-year. [ViSenze Case Study](https://www.visenze.com/resource-centre/myntra-increases-its-visual-image-search-adoption-by-35yoy/)

- **Zalora** (Southeast Asia): Implemented ViSenze's search and discovery solution, achieving over 10% higher engagement rate and 15% increase in average order value. The implementation solved language barrier challenges for shoppers, with relevant product results appearing in under 500ms. [ViSenze Case Study](https://www.visenze.com/resource-centre/zalora-shortens-path-to-purchase-with-smart-product-discovery/)

- **Meesho**: Deployed AI-powered visual search to serve Gen Z and tech-savvy shoppers, achieving significantly higher conversion rates compared to text search.

- **Reliance AJIO** (India): Implemented visual search to enable seamless search experiences for Gen Z shoppers, converting at higher rates than text search.

- **Showpo** (Australia): Uses "See Similar" carousel powered by ViSenze to curate visually similar items based on product page attributes.

**Pricing Model:** SaaS subscription with API call volume components. Public pricing not published; enterprise quotes required. The company offers a 30-day free trial with ROI typically achieved within 2 weeks of implementation.

**Value Chain Stage:** E-commerce discovery (primary), with extensions into merchandising and buying through automated tagging.

---

### 1.2 Syte

**Overview:** Syte positions itself as the "#1 Product Discovery Platform for Apparel Ecommerce," offering visual search, AI tagging, and hyper-personalization specifically built for fashion, home decor, and jewelry.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

Syte serves major fashion retailers globally, though specific brand case studies are not extensively published. The platform focuses on:

- **Visual Discovery**: Image search, inspiration gallery, visual recommendation engines (Shop Similar, Shop Social, Shop the Look)
- **AI Tagging & Merchandising**: Automatic product tagging to drive revenue and optimize text search
- **Hyper-Personalization**: Individualized product recommendations

**Pricing Model:** SaaS subscription. Pricing not publicly available; enterprise quotes required. The platform emphasizes "no contracts, cancel anytime" in some go-to-market materials.

**Value Chain Stage:** E-commerce discovery (primary), merchandising and buying (through AI tagging).

---

### 1.3 True Fit

**Overview:** True Fit provides AI-powered fit and sizing intelligence for retailers, operating what it describes as "the Fit Intelligence Layer for Agentic Commerce." The company claims 65% market share in size and fit solutions.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

- **Lands End**: Implemented True Fit to decrease returns and bracket buying. According to their Senior Digital Merchandising Marketing, "We know from the data that finding the right fit drives consumer loyalty and increases customer conversion."

- **Princess Polly**: Utilizing True Fit data to learn about shopper fit preferences, driving customer value.

- **Moosejaw**: Reduced fit-related returns by 24% by targeting size bracketing behavior.

**Reported Outcomes:**
- Up to 40% reduction in fit-related returns when guidance is followed
- 1-2% site-wide conversion lift (measured against control groups)
- Platform covers 80M+ active users, 540M+ products, $616B in annual transaction value

**Pricing Model:** SaaS subscription with enterprise licensing. Pricing not publicly published. The company operates on a per-implementation basis with volume considerations.

**Value Chain Stage:** Post-purchase and returns (primary), e-commerce discovery at product page (size recommendation on PDP).

---

### 1.4 Heuritech

**Overview:** Founded in 2013 by two PhDs in Machine Learning, Heuritech provides AI-based visual recognition and trend forecasting by analyzing social media images. The company claims to analyze millions of social images monthly.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

Heuritech works with apparel brands for trend forecasting:

- **Trend Quantification**: Analyzes social media images to quantify what people wear, detecting 2000 fashion attributes from macro (prints, colors, fabrics) to granular (shapes, attributes)
- **Predictive Insights**: Provides forecasts on prints, colors, fabrics, silhouettes for upcoming seasons
- **Collaboration with Première Vision**: Partnership to combine AI-powered trend forecasting with trade show ecosystem

**Pricing Model:** SaaS platform or API delivery. Enterprise quotes required; no public pricing published.

**Value Chain Stage:** Trend forecasting (primary), design and sourcing (through predictive insights).

---

### 1.5 Stylitics

**Overview:** Stylitics provides "Inspirational Commerce" solutions focusing on outfitting and product bundling across the shopper journey. The company powers experiences for 175+ retailers and reaches 200M+ shoppers monthly.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

- **J.Crew & Madewell**: Made editorial imagery instantly shoppable. [Case Study](https://stylitics.com/resources/case-studies/how-j-crew-madewell-made-editorial-imagery-instantly-shoppable/)

The platform provides:
- AI-powered product bundling on every product detail page (PDP)
- Personalized outfit recommendations
- Cross-category bundling
- Style at scale with 100% catalog coverage

**Reported Outcomes (Forrester TEI Study):**
- 563% average ROI over 3-year period
- 10% average order value increase
- 15% conversion rate increase
- $1M+ saved on image processing costs

**Pricing Model:** SaaS subscription. Pricing not publicly available; enterprise agreements required.

**Value Chain Stage:** E-commerce discovery (primary), post-purchase (through styling recommendations).

---

### 1.6 Tagalys

**Overview:** Tagalys provides smart visual merchandising for fashion on Shopify, offering search, recommendations, and analytics specifically designed for fashion and lifestyle brands.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

Serving fashion brands worldwide (specific names not extensively published), Tagalys provides:

- **Visual Aisles**: Digital showrooms for brand/trade stories
- **Search That Learns from Fashion**: Fashion-aware search implementation
- **Product Recommendations**: Curated recommendations for awareness-to-consideration bridging
- **Analytics**: Fashion-specific insights beyond standard GA4

**Reported Outcomes:**
- 65% time saved on merchandising tasks
- 25% revenue increase
- 30% higher average order value
- 86.2% uptime SLA

**Pricing Model:** SaaS subscription. 28-day free trial, "No Contracts, Cancel anytime." Pricing not fully published publicly.

**Value Chain Stage:** Merchandising and buying (primary), e-commerce discovery (search and recommendations).

---

### 1.7 WGSN

**Overview:** WGSN (Worth Global Style Network) is one of the most established names in fashion trend forecasting, now incorporating AI and data analytics into its traditional forecasting methodology.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

WGSN serves the global fashion industry with:

- Trend forecasting reports and analytics
- Consumer insights
- Market intelligence
- AI-enhanced predictive capabilities

**Pricing Model:** Enterprise subscription (report-based). Pricing varies by tier and access level; not publicly published in detail.

**Value Chain Stage:** Trend forecasting (primary), design and sourcing (through trend intelligence).

---

### 1.8 ReturnLogic

**Overview:** ReturnLogic provides returns management software for ecommerce brands, helping streamline the returns process and reduce costs associated with returns.

**Deployed Brands & Use Cases:**

- **Artisan Shoe Brand** (case study): Saved 80 hours per week with RMA automation

**Reported Outcomes:**
- Decrease in returns (specific percentage varies)
- Decrease in time processing returns
- Increase in profit per return

**Pricing Model:** SaaS subscription. Specific pricing not published; quotes required.

**Value Chain Stage:** Post-purchase and returns (exclusive focus).

---

### 1.9 ViSenze (also covers inventory/search in other contexts)

The ViSenze platform also supports:
- **Inventory search in back office**: Through product tagging and catalog management
- **Social search**: Making social media posts shoppable

---

## 2. Pricing Model Summary

| Solution | Pricing Model | Public Pricing | Notes |
|----------|---------------|----------------|-------|
| ViSenze | SaaS + API calls | No | 30-day free trial; quotes required |
| Syte | SaaS subscription | No | No contracts option available |
| True Fit | Enterprise SaaS | No | Volume-based enterprise licensing |
| Heuritech | SaaS/API | No | Platform or API delivery |
| Stylitics | SaaS subscription | No | Enterprise agreements required |
| Tagalys | SaaS subscription | No | 28-day free trial; no contracts |
| WGSN | Enterprise subscription | No | Tiered by access level |
| ReturnLogic | SaaS subscription | No | Quotes required |

**Key Finding:** None of the identified solutions publish specific pricing publicly. All operate on quote-based enterprise models, with some offering entry-level trials or subscriptions.

---

## 3. Value Chain Mapping

| Solution | Trend Forecasting | Design & Sourcing | Merchandising & Buying | E-commerce Discovery | In-Store Experience | Post-Purchase & Returns |
|----------|-------------------|-------------------|------------------------|---------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
| **ViSenze** | | | ● | ●●● | | |
| **Syte** | | | ● | ●●● | | |
| **True Fit** | | | | ● | | ●●● |
| **Heuritech** | ●●● | ● | | | | |
| **WGSN** | ●●● | ● | | | | |
| **Stylitics** | | | | ●●● | | ● |
| **Tagalys** | | | ●●● | ●● | | |
| **ReturnLogic** | | | | | | ●●● |

**Legend:** ● = present, ●●● = primary focus

### Observations on Multi-Stage Coverage:

1. **ViSenze, Syte**: Extended from e-commerce discovery into merchandising through product tagging capabilities—organic product evolution
2. **True Fit**: Spans PDP (e-commerce) and returns through fit prediction—organic design
3. **Heuritech, WGSN**: Stay focused on trend forecasting with minor extension to design/sourcing—core competency focus
4. **Stylitics**: Spans discovery and post-purchase through outfit recommendations—organic expansion

---

## 4. Differentiation Dimensions

### 4.1 Primary Competitive Dimensions

**1. Visual Recognition & Matching Capability**
- ViSenze, Syte, and Stylitics compete on visual search accuracy, recommendation relevance, and image understanding depth
- Differentiation through training data scale, attribute detection granularity (ViSenze claims 2000+ fashion attributes)

**2. Data Network Effects**
- True Fit's competitive advantage stems from 80M+ active users and $616B transaction data—difficult to replicate
- Heuritech's advantage: millions of social images analyzed monthly
- Network effects create defensible moats

**3. Fit Intelligence & Personalization**
- True Fit owns the fit/sizing category with 65% market share claim
- Competes on personalization depth (individual shopper profiles vs. generic recommendations)
- Integration with AI agents represents new frontier

### 4.2 Underserved Segments

**In-Store Experience** is notably underserved:
- Most solutions focus on digital/e-commerce channels
- Limited AI solutions for in-store product finding, clienteling, or inventory lookup
- Opportunity for solutions bridging online/offline inventory search

**Back-Office Inventory Search** is minimally addressed:
- While product tagging exists, dedicated inventory search solutions for warehouse/buying teams are sparse
- Could be served by existing players expanding from merchandising

**Design & Sourcing** remains lightly served:
- Heuritech and WGSN address trend forecasting
- Limited AI solutions for supplier discovery, cost optimization, or production planning in fashion

---

## 5. Key Takeaways

1. **Pricing is uniformly opaque**: All solutions require enterprise quotes; no SAAS pricing公开发布
2. **E-commerce discovery is saturated**: Multiple well-funded players (ViSenze, Syte, Stylitics, Tagalys) compete
3. **Fit/sizing is consolidated**: True Fit's 65% market share claim suggests limited competition
4. **Trend forecasting is mature**: Traditional players (WGSN) now compete with AI-native entrants (Heuritech)
5. **Post-purchase is emerging**: Returns management (ReturnLogic) and fit prediction (True Fit) growing faster than other segments
6. **In-store remains blue ocean**: Significant opportunity for vertical AI solutions in physical retail

---

*Report compiled: March 2026*

*Sources: Company websites, case studies, product documentation, and public disclosures. Pricing information based on available public materials; analyst estimates or customer disclosures unavailable for this report.*"}

function.name: browser
function.description: Control the browser via OpenClaw's browser control server (status/start/stop/profiles/tabs/open/snapshot/screenshot/actions). Browser choice: omit profile by default for the isolated OpenClaw-managed browser (`openclaw`). For the logged-in user browser on the local host, use profile="user". A supported Chromium-based browser (v144+) must be running. Use only when existing logins/cookies matter and the user is present. When a node-hosted browser proxy is available, the tool may auto-route to it. Pin a node with node=<id|name> or target="node". When using refs from snapshot (e.g. e12), keep the same tab: prefer passing targetId from the snapshot response into subsequent actions (act/click/type/etc). For stable, self-resolving refs across calls, use snapshot with refs="aria" (Playwright aria-ref ids). Default refs="role" are role+name-based. Use snapshot+act for UI automation. Avoid act:wait by default; use only in exceptional cases when no reliable UI state exists. target selects browser location (sandbox|host|node). Default: host. Host target allowed.

function.name: cron
function.description:
Manage Gateway cron jobs (status/list/add/update/remove/run/runs) and send wake events.

ACTIONS:
- status: Check cron scheduler status
- list: List jobs (use includeDisabled:true to include disabled)
- add: Create job (requires job object, see schema below)
- update: Modify job (requires jobId + patch object)
- remove: Delete job (requires jobId)
- run: Trigger job immediately (requires jobId)
- runs: Get job run history (requires jobId)
- wake: Send wake event (requires text, optional mode)

JOB SCHEMA (for add action):
{
  "name": "string (optional)",
  "schedule": { ... },      // Required: when to run
  "payload": { ... },       // Required: what to execute
  "delivery": { ... },      // Optional: announce summary (isolated/current/session:xxx only) or webhook POST
  "sessionTarget": "main" | "isolated" | "current" | "session:<custom-id>",  // Optional, defaults based on context
  "enabled": true | false   // Optional, default true
}

SESSION TARGET OPTIONS:
- "main": Run in the main session (requires payload.kind="systemEvent")
- "isolated": Run in an ephemeral isolated session (requires payload.kind="agentTurn")
- "current": Bind to the current session where the cron is created (resolved at creation time)
- "session:<custom-id>": Run in a persistent named session (e.g., "session:project-alpha-daily")

DEFAULT BEHAVIOR (unchanged for backward compatibility):
- payload.kind="systemEvent" → defaults to "main"
- payload.kind="agentTurn" → defaults to "isolated"
To use current session binding, explicitly set sessionTarget="current".

SCHEDULE TYPES (schedule.kind):
- "at": One-shot at absolute time
  { "kind": "at", "at": "<ISO-8601 timestamp>" }
- "every": Recurring interval
  { "kind": "every", "everyMs": <interval-ms>, "anchorMs": <optional-start-ms> }
- "cron": Cron expression
  { "kind": "cron", "expr": "<cron-expression>", "tz": "<optional-timezone>" }

ISO timestamps without an explicit timezone are treated as UTC.

PAYLOAD TYPES (payload.kind):
- "systemEvent": Injects text as system event into session
  { "kind": "systemEvent", "text": "<message>" }
- "agentTurn": Runs agent with message (isolated sessions only)
  { "kind": "agentTurn", "message": "<prompt>", "model": "<optional>", "thinking": "<optional>", "timeoutSeconds": <optional, 0 means no timeout> }

DELIVERY (top-level):
  { "mode": "none|announce|webhook", "channel": "<optional>", "to": "<optional>", "bestEffort": <optional-bool> }
  - Default for isolated agentTurn jobs (when delivery omitted): "announce"
  - announce: send to chat channel (optional channel/to target)
  - webhook: send finished-run event as HTTP POST to delivery.to (URL required)
  - If the task needs to send to a specific chat/recipient, set announce delivery.channel/to; do not call messaging tools inside the run.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:
- sessionTarget="main" REQUIRES payload.kind="systemEvent"
- sessionTarget="isolated" | "current" | "session:xxx" REQUIRES payload.kind="agentTurn"
- For webhook callbacks, use delivery.mode="webhook" with delivery.to set to a URL.
Default: prefer isolated agentTurn jobs unless the user explicitly wants current-session binding.

WAKE MODES (for wake action):
- "next-heartbeat" (default): Wake on next heartbeat
- "now": Wake immediately

Use jobId as the canonical identifier; id is accepted for compatibility. Use contextMessages (0-10) to add previous messages as context to the job text.
